title: WISeKey and SEALSQ Establish Quantisimo Corp. and Execute Nasdaq SPAC Listing Intent with GigCapital8 - Quantum Computing Report
gist: WISeKey and SEALSQ have created Quantisimo Corp., a joint SPV targeting a Nasdaq listing via SPAC merger with GigCapital8 at a $575M pre-money valuation, with ambitions to scale to $2B through acquisition of up to five additional quantum companies. The platform is explicitly designed as a post-quantum cryptography pure-play, capitalizing on Trump's June 22, 2026 executive order mandating domestic quantum development. The deal targets Q1 2027 close.

insights.claim: Quantisimo Corp., the WISeKey/SEALSQ joint SPV, has signed a non-binding LOI with GigCapital8 SPAC at a $575 million initial pre-money enterprise value, with a stated target to scale to $2 billion through acquisition of up to five additional quantum companies.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: The $575M-to-$2B valuation expansion plan via roll-up strategy signals aggressive quantum sector consolidation funded through public markets; investors should assess execution risk against SPAC dilution.
insights.quote: assigns the combined corporate entity an initial pre-money enterprise value of approximately $575 million
insights.locator: SPAC Business Combination and Multi-Company Acquisition Strategy

insights.claim: The Quantisimo/GigCapital8 business combination is projected to close in Q1 2027, pending regulatory review, giving investors a roughly 6-9 month timeline to the Nasdaq listing.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: The Q1 2027 target close date sets a definitive timeline for investors to evaluate SPAC arbitrage or merger-related position sizing.
insights.quote: currently projected to finalize regulatory reviews and close during the first quarter of 2027
insights.locator: SPAC Business Combination and Multi-Company Acquisition Strategy

insights.claim: WISeKey CEO Carlos Creus Moreira and GigCapital Global Founder Dr. Avi Katz will jointly lead the operational strategy for Quantisimo, combining enterprise cybersecurity expertise with SPAC capital markets experience.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: The leadership pairing of a cybersecurity operator with a SPAC specialist signals the platform is optimized for public market debut, not purely technical R&D.
insights.quote: Under the operational guidance of WISeKey CEO Carlos Creus Moreira and GigCapital Global Founder Dr. Avi Katz
insights.locator: SPAC Business Combination and Multi-Company Acquisition Strategy

insights.claim: Quantisimo's core technology assets include quantum-resistant cryptographic software and post-quantum semiconductors from SEALSQ's SealQuantum.com portfolio, specifically designed to replace legacy RSA and Elliptic Curve Cryptography (ECC) systems.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: The explicit targeting of RSA/ECC replacement positions Quantisimo in the highest-urgency segment of post-quantum security, driven by NIST standardization timelines and government mandates.
insights.quote: quantum-resistant cryptographic software models and post-quantum semiconductors designed to replace legacy public-key frameworks like RSA and Elliptic Curve Cryptography (ECC)
insights.locator: Pure-Play Investment Exposure and Asset Contributions

insights.claim: SEALSQ's post-quantum security products target multi-factor authentication tokens, smart grid infrastructure, and IT network equipment as primary deployment verticals for RSA/ECC replacement.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: These deployment verticals span both consumer enterprise and critical infrastructure, indicating a broad total addressable market for post-quantum hardware.
insights.quote: multi-factor authentication tokens, smart grid infrastructure, and IT network equipment
insights.locator: Pure-Play Investment Exposure and Asset Contributions

insights.claim: Quantisimo is structured as a 'Trusted Quantum Pure-Play' to give public market investors concentrated exposure to the quantum economy without dilution from non-quantum business lines.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: The pure-play framing is a deliberate differentiation from diversified tech conglomerates, appealing to thematic ETF inclusion and quantum-focused institutional allocators.
insights.quote: Quantisimo is architected to operate as a "Trusted Quantum Pure-Play" vehicle, providing public market investors with a direct, concentrated financial gateway into the evolving quantum economy
insights.locator: Pure-Play Investment Exposure and Asset Contributions

themes.theme: Federal Policy Catalyst: Trump Quantum Executive Order

insights.claim: President Trump signed an executive order titled 'Ushering in the Next Frontier of Quantum Innovation' on June 22, 2026, mandating accelerated domestic development in quantum computing, secure networking, sensing arrays, and sovereign supply chains.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: The executive order creates direct procurement and regulatory tailwinds for domestic quantum companies like Quantisimo; investors should monitor implementation rulemaking timelines.
insights.quote: federal executive order, "Ushering in the Next Frontier of Quantum Innovation," signed by President Donald J. Trump on Monday, June 22, 2026
insights.locator: Federal Macro Policy and Market Positioning

insights.claim: Quantisimo's formation directly cites the Trump quantum executive order as a macro policy driver, explicitly linking federal sovereign supply chain mandates to anticipated expansion of commercial procurement opportunities for military and enterprise hardware.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Explicit policy linkage suggests Quantisimo is positioning for government contracts; investors should evaluate the credibility of this defense-adjacent revenue path.
insights.quote: The resulting public-private partnership momentum is expected to directly expand global commercial procurement opportunities for Quantisimo's military and enterprise-grade hardware components.
insights.locator: Federal Macro Policy and Market Positioning

insights.claim: The Trump quantum executive order mandates accelerated development of 'sovereign supply chains' for quantum technology, directly benefiting domestically incorporated quantum hardware companies over foreign competitors.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Sovereign supply chain mandates could function like CHIPS Act provisions, creating structural advantages for US-listed quantum hardware entities and disadvantaging foreign-domiciled suppliers.
insights.quote: localized sensing arrays, and sovereign supply chains
insights.locator: Federal Macro Policy and Market Positioning

themes.theme: Quantum Sector Roll-Up and Consolidation Strategy

insights.claim: Quantisimo plans to acquire up to five additional quantum companies as part of a build-up consolidation program, targeting a scale from $575M to $2B enterprise value through inorganic growth.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: A 3.5x valuation expansion via M&A roll-up in a nascent sector carries significant integration and dilution risk, but also positions Quantisimo as a potential acquirer of distressed or early-stage quantum startups.
insights.quote: targeting and absorbing up to five additional innovative quantum companies
insights.locator: SPAC Business Combination and Multi-Company Acquisition Strategy

insights.claim: Unlike single-methodology quantum engineering firms, Quantisimo plans to consolidate multiple quantum architectures and intellectual property portfolios, positioning as a technology-agnostic aggregator across the quantum stack.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Technology-agnostic aggregation is a hedge against quantum modality uncertainty (superconducting vs. photonic vs. trapped ion), but may also dilute technical depth and create integration complexity.
insights.quote: Unlike firms bounded by a single engineering method, the asset pool will consolidate internally developed architectures alongside intellectual property and strategic interests
insights.locator: Pure-Play Investment Exposure and Asset Contributions

insights.claim: WISeKey International Holding Ltd. is described as a cybersecurity firm, and SEALSQ Corp. is its semiconductor-focused subsidiary; Quantisimo Corp. is a jointly owned SPV formed specifically for the SPAC transaction and quantum roll-up.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: The SPV structure separates quantum assets from WISeKey's broader cybersecurity operations, enabling cleaner valuation and targeted capital raising without diluting the parent company's existing business.
insights.quote: Cybersecurity firm WISeKey International Holding Ltd. and its semiconductor-focused subsidiary SEALSQ Corp. have announced the creation of a jointly owned special purpose vehicle, Quantisimo Corp.

insights.claim: GigCapital8 Corp. is described as a publicly traded Private-to-Public Equity (PPE) special purpose acquisition company, indicating it is an operational SPAC vehicle already listed on Nasdaq, giving Quantisimo an established shell for reverse merger.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Using an already-listed SPAC accelerates the path to Nasdaq versus a traditional IPO, reducing time-to-market but maintaining SPAC-specific risks around shareholder redemption and earnout structures.
insights.quote: GigCapital8 Corp., a publicly traded Private-to-Public Equity (PPE) special purpose acquisition company (SPAC)
